chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: The Fetch API provides a modern way to make HTTP requests in JavaScript.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: Fetch returns a Promise that resolves to the Response object.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: The basic syntax is fetch URL then response then data catch error.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: The response.json method parses the response body as JSON.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: You can use fetch to get data from any public API on the internet.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: Let me show you an example using a free weather API.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: First, we call fetch with the API URL including our parameters.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: Then we chain .then to convert the response to JSON.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: Then we chain another .then to process the actual data.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: We can display this data on our webpage using DOM manipulation.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: For POST requests, you need to pass an options object as the second parameter.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: The options object includes method, headers, and body properties.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: The body should be converted to JSON string using JSON.stringify.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: Headers should include Content-Type application/json for JSON data.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: Using async await with fetch makes the code cleaner and easier to read.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: Error handling is important. Always handle network errors and non-OK responses.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: CORS, Cross-Origin Resource Sharing, is a security feature you might encounter.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: CORS blocks requests from different origins unless the server allows it.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: You can use a CORS proxy for development, but the server should handle CORS in production.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: APIs are how modern web applications communicate and share data.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: In your projects, you will frequently use fetch to get and send data to servers.

chunks.title: JavaScript Fetch API & Working with APIs
chunks.text: Practice making API calls as it is an essential skill for web developers.

text: The Fetch API provides a modern way to make HTTP requests in JavaScript. Fetch returns a Promise that resolves to the Response object. The basic syntax is fetch URL then response then data catch error. The response.json method parses the response body as JSON. You can use fetch to get data from any public API on the internet. Let me show you an example using a free weather API. First, we call fetch with the API URL including our parameters. Then we chain .then to convert the response to JSON. Then we chain another .then to process the actual data. We can display this data on our webpage using DOM manipulation. For POST requests, you need to pass an options object as the second parameter. The options object includes method, headers, and body properties. The body should be converted to JSON string using JSON.stringify. Headers should include Content-Type application/json for JSON data. Using async await with fetch makes the code cleaner and easier to read. Error handling is important. Always handle network errors and non-OK responses. CORS, Cross-Origin Resource Sharing, is a security feature you might encounter. CORS blocks requests from different origins unless the server allows it. You can use a CORS proxy for development, but the server should handle CORS in production. APIs are how modern web applications communicate and share data. In your projects, you will frequently use fetch to get and send data to servers. Practice making API calls as it is an essential skill for web developers.
